proposed_hypotheses.text: PFS distribution in ds001_breast is unimodal with mean near 5 months and a long right tail; baseline PFS varies in a clinically meaningful way (>1 month) across ECOG performance status (0/1/2).

analyses.result_summary: PFS summary: mean=4.69, median=4.54 months, sd=2.50, range [0.00, 16.66]. ECOG-stratified mean PFS: ECOG=0: n=17592, mean=5.64, ECOG=1: n=24971, mean=4.44, ECOG=2: n=7437, mean=3.29. One-way ANOVA across ECOG: F=2860.7, p=0. PFS drops monotonically by >1 month per ECOG step.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope) in this cohort.
Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0 patients.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ age_years: slope=0.1759 months/year, p=0 (n=50000). R^2=0.492.
Welch t-test pfs_months by sex_female: female mean=4.686, male mean=4.699, diff=-0.013 months, p=0.562.
OLS pfs_months ~ ecog_ps: slope=-1.180 months/ECOG-unit, p=0. ECOG is a strong inverse predictor of PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without brain metastases.
Patients with node_positive=1 have shorter pfs_months than node-negative patients.
Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: stage_iv=1 mean PFS=3.62 vs stage_iv=0 mean=5.15, diff=-1.54 months, p=0.
Welch t-test: has_brain_mets=1 mean PFS=3.75 vs absent mean=4.80, diff=-1.05 months, p=1.45e-178.
Welch t-test: node_positive=1 mean PFS=4.68 vs node-negative mean=4.70, diff=-0.024 months, p=0.292.
OLS pfs_months ~ tumor_size_cm: slope=0.0006 months/cm, p=0.931.

proposed_hypotheses.text: er_positive=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than ER- patients.
pr_positive=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than PR- patients.
her2_positive=1 patients have shorter mean pfs_months than HER2-negative patients.
her2_low=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than non-HER2-low patients.
postmenopausal=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than premenopausal patients.
Higher ki67_pct (proliferation) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test pfs_months by er_positive: pos mean=4.865 (n=34886), neg mean=4.297 (n=15114), diff=0.568, p=3.7e-143
Welch t-test pfs_months by pr_positive: pos mean=4.824 (n=31954), neg mean=4.462 (n=18046), diff=0.362, p=5.82e-59
Welch t-test pfs_months by her2_positive: pos mean=4.317 (n=9060), neg mean=4.776 (n=40940), diff=-0.459, p=6.1e-70
Welch t-test pfs_months by her2_low: pos mean=4.765 (n=18543), neg mean=4.650 (n=31457), diff=0.114, p=1.04e-06
Welch t-test pfs_months by postmenopausal: pos mean=4.684 (n=30238), neg mean=4.707 (n=19762), diff=-0.023, p=0.311
OLS pfs_months ~ ki67_pct: slope=-0.01876 months per percentage point, p=2.98e-93.

proposed_hypotheses.text: brca1_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than non-mutated patients.
brca2_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than non-mutated patients.
pik3ca_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than non-mutated patients.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test pfs_months by brca1_mutation: pos mean=4.669 (n=1277), neg mean=4.694 (n=48723), diff=-0.024, p=0.724
Welch t-test pfs_months by brca2_mutation: pos mean=4.569 (n=1222), neg mean=4.696 (n=48778), diff=-0.127, p=0.0813
Welch t-test pfs_months by pik3ca_mutation: pos mean=4.328 (n=17365), neg mean=4.887 (n=32635), diff=-0.559, p=6.07e-144

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl (better nutrition/liver synthetic function) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l (tumor burden marker) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l (often a marker of bone/liver involvement) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Liver enzymes (ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl) are inversely associated with pfs_months.
Renal markers (creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl) are inversely associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ albumin_g_dl: slope=0.49877 per unit, p=9.39e-112, n=50000, R^2=0.0100
OLS pfs_months ~ ldh_u_l: slope=-0.00051 per unit, p=0.000245, n=50000, R^2=0.0003
OLS pfs_months ~ alkaline_phosphatase_u_l: slope=-0.00067 per unit, p=0.00295, n=50000, R^2=0.0002

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher inflammatory markers (crp_mg_l, nlr) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Electrolyte derangements (sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) have detectable associations with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ hemoglobin_g_dl: slope=-0.00639 per unit, p=0.304, n=50000, R^2=0.0000
OLS pfs_months ~ weight_loss_pct_6mo: slope=-0.07473 per unit, p=4.07e-148, n=50000, R^2=0.0133

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_tamoxifen (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.
treatment_palbociclib (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.
treatment_trastuzumab (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.
treatment_olaparib (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.
treatment_pembrolizumab (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test treatment_tamoxifen: treated mean=4.723 (n=15035) vs untreated mean=4.680 (n=34965), diff=0.043, p=0.0769.
Welch t-test treatment_palbociclib: treated mean=5.408 (n=17428) vs untreated mean=4.310 (n=32572), diff=1.098, p=0.
Welch t-test treatment_trastuzumab: treated mean=4.645 (n=10039) vs untreated mean=4.705 (n=39961), diff=-0.060, p=0.0292.
Welch t-test treatment_olaparib: treated mean=4.660 (n=4946) vs untreated mean=4.696 (n=45054), diff=-0.036, p=0.33.
Welch t-test treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan: treated mean=4.686 (n=4987) vs untreated mean=4.694 (n=45013), diff=-0.007, p=0.846.
Welch t-test treatment_pembrolizumab: treated mean=4.630 (n=7585) vs untreated mean=4.704 (n=42415), diff=-0.074, p=0.0175.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_tamoxifen effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS coef on treatment_tamoxifen = 0.004 months, SE=0.013, p=0.789 (model R^2=0.706).
Adjusted OLS coef on treatment_palbociclib = 1.100 months, SE=0.012, p=0 (model R^2=0.750).
Adjusted OLS coef on treatment_trastuzumab = -0.015 months, SE=0.015, p=0.317 (model R^2=0.706).
Adjusted OLS coef on treatment_olaparib = -0.027 months, SE=0.020, p=0.183 (model R^2=0.706).
Adjusted OLS coef on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan = 0.024 months, SE=0.020, p=0.237 (model R^2=0.706).
Adjusted OLS coef on treatment_pembrolizumab = -0.035 months, SE=0.017, p=0.0366 (model R^2=0.706).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS over all features and treatments will show ECOG, stage IV, has_brain_mets, albumin (positive), LDH (negative), HER2 status, ER status, and treatment_palbociclib as among the largest-magnitude predictors of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Full OLS (all features + all treatments) R^2=0.792, n=50000. Lowest-p features: stage_iv: coef=-1.549, p=0; has_brain_mets: coef=-0.983, p=0; pik3ca_mutation: coef=-0.582, p=0; ecog_ps: coef=-1.171, p=0; age_years: coef=0.176, p=0; ki67_pct: coef=-0.019, p=0; albumin_g_dl: coef=0.476, p=0; weight_loss_pct_6mo: coef=-0.077, p=0; treatment_palbociclib: coef=1.101, p=0; er_positive: coef=0.532, p=8.6e-275; her2_positive: coef=-0.471, p=2.4e-239; ldh_u_l: coef=-0.001, p=2.2e-16; brca1_mutation: coef=-0.068, p=0.036; treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan: coef=0.033, p=0.049; node_positive: coef=-0.018, p=0.081; treatment_olaparib: coef=-0.026, p=0.12; potassium_meq_l: coef=0.014, p=0.16.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_tamoxifen interacts with er_positive: tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months only in er_positive=1 patients.
treatment_palbociclib interacts with er_positive: palbociclib prolongs pfs_months only in er_positive=1 patients.
treatment_trastuzumab interacts with her2_positive: trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months only in her2_positive=1 patients.
treatment_olaparib interacts with brca1_mutation: olaparib prolongs pfs_months in brca1_mutation=1 patients.
treatment_olaparib interacts with brca2_mutation: olaparib prolongs pfs_months in brca2_mutation=1 patients.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan interacts with her2_low: sacituzumab govitecan prolongs pfs_months in her2_low=1 patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab interacts with her2_positive: pembrolizumab pfs_months effect differs by her2_positive status.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS with treatment × modifier interaction. Interaction coef=0.005 (p=0.846); main treatment coef=-0.003 (p=0.912).
Adjusted OLS with treatment × modifier interaction. Interaction coef=1.582 (p=0); main treatment coef=-0.007 (p=0.713).
Adjusted OLS with treatment × modifier interaction. Interaction coef=0.028 (p=0.467); main treatment coef=-0.016 (p=0.33).
Adjusted OLS with treatment × modifier interaction. Interaction coef=0.202 (p=0.124); main treatment coef=-0.032 (p=0.119).
Adjusted OLS with treatment × modifier interaction. Interaction coef=-0.035 (p=0.79); main treatment coef=-0.026 (p=0.202).
Adjusted OLS with treatment × modifier interaction. Interaction coef=0.035 (p=0.401); main treatment coef=0.011 (p=0.651).
Adjusted OLS with treatment × modifier interaction. Interaction coef=0.044 (p=0.314); main treatment coef=-0.043 (p=0.0211).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_tamoxifen recipients have longer pfs_months than non-recipients.
Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib recipients have longer pfs_months than non-recipients.
Within her2_positive=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab recipients have longer pfs_months than non-recipients.
Within brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib recipients have longer pfs_months than non-recipients.
Within her2_low=1 patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan recipients have longer pfs_months than non-recipients.
Treatment-by-ECOG interactions exist for at least one treatment, indicating performance-status-dependent benefit.
Treatment-by-stage_iv interactions exist (treatment effect varies between stage IV and earlier stage).
Treatment-by-postmenopausal interactions exist for hormonally targeted therapies (tamoxifen, palbociclib).
Treatment-by-brain_mets interactions exist (treatment effect varies in patients with vs. without brain metastases).

analyses.result_summary: Strongest treatment × ecog_ps interaction: treatment_pembrolizumab_x_ecog_ps (interaction coef=-0.052, p=0.0351).
Strongest treatment × stage_iv interaction: treatment_trastuzumab_x_stage_iv (interaction coef=0.055, p=0.0958).
Strongest treatment × postmenopausal interaction: treatment_olaparib_x_postmenopausal (interaction coef=0.078, p=0.0611).
Strongest treatment × has_brain_mets interaction: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan_x_has_brain_mets (interaction coef=0.147, p=0.0305).

proposed_hypotheses.text: For treatment_tamoxifen, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.

For treatment_palbociclib, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.

For treatment_trastuzumab, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.

For treatment_olaparib, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.

For treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.

For treatment_pembrolizumab, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction screen for treatment_tamoxifen, top 5 modifiers (lowest interaction p): stage_iv: int_coef=-0.047, p=0.104; creatinine_mg_dl: int_coef=-0.063, p=0.153; sex_female: int_coef=-0.037, p=0.159; brca1_mutation: int_coef=0.113, p=0.182; ast_u_l: int_coef=-0.001, p=0.25.

Interaction screen for treatment_palbociclib, top 5 modifiers (lowest interaction p): er_positive: int_coef=1.582, p=0; pr_positive: int_coef=1.036, p=0; her2_positive: int_coef=-1.348, p=0; pik3ca_mutation: int_coef=-1.679, p=0; ki67_pct: int_coef=-0.053, p=0.

Interaction screen for treatment_trastuzumab, top 5 modifiers (lowest interaction p): stage_iv: int_coef=0.055, p=0.0958; hemoglobin_g_dl: int_coef=0.012, p=0.144; ki67_pct: int_coef=0.002, p=0.168; node_positive: int_coef=-0.039, p=0.192; brca2_mutation: int_coef=-0.117, p=0.23.

Interaction screen for treatment_olaparib, top 5 modifiers (lowest interaction p): postmenopausal: int_coef=0.078, p=0.0611; alt_u_l: int_coef=0.003, p=0.0629; calcium_mg_dl: int_coef=0.066, p=0.102; brca1_mutation: int_coef=0.202, p=0.124; bun_mg_dl: int_coef=-0.005, p=0.188.

Interaction screen for treatment_pembrolizumab, top 5 modifiers (lowest interaction p): ecog_ps: int_coef=-0.052, p=0.0351; er_positive: int_coef=-0.065, p=0.069; potassium_meq_l: int_coef=0.056, p=0.0975; pik3ca_mutation: int_coef=0.053, p=0.127; node_positive: int_coef=-0.048, p=0.156.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is positive and large only in er_positive=1 patients (effect ~+1.6 months) and ~zero in er_positive=0 patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months becomes more negative as ecog_ps increases (worse PS, more harm/no benefit).
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan effect on pfs_months differs by creatinine_mg_dl (interaction p<0.05 in screen).
treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months is more positive in postmenopausal=1 patients than in postmenopausal=0 patients (top modifier from screen).

analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib top modifier=er_positive (int_p=0). ER+ effect=1.577 (n=34886, p=0); ER- effect=-0.017 (n=15114, p=0.627).
Pembro top modifier=ecog_ps (int_p=0.0351). ECOG>=median effect=-0.126 (n=32408, p=0.000702); ECOG<median effect=-0.008 (n=17592, p=0.872).
Sacit top modifier=creatinine (int_p=0.0106). Hi-creat effect=-0.018 (n=25456, p=0.731); Lo-creat effect=0.004 (n=24544, p=0.944).
Olap top modifier=postmenopausal (int_p=0.0611). Postmeno effect=-0.028 (n=30238, p=0.555); Premeno effect=-0.049 (n=19762, p=0.414).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib benefit is concentrated where her2_positive=0 (i.e., the ER+/HER2- subgroup).
treatment_olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated patients is similar in BRCA1+ and BRCA2+ subgroups.
treatment_tamoxifen benefit, if present, is restricted to ER+ postmenopausal patients.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan benefit is concentrated in HER2-low AND ER- (TNBC-like) patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) patients.

analyses.result_summary: ER+/HER2- palbo: n=28589, n_treated=9971, eff=1.924, p=0

BRCA1+ olaparib: BRCA1: n=1277, n_treated=120, eff=0.398, p=0.117; BRCA2+ olaparib: BRCA2: n=1222, n_treated=119, eff=0.295, p=0.192; BRCA+ <50yo: BRCA<50: n=173, n_treated=15, eff=0.053, p=0.898; BRCA+ postmeno: BRCA postmeno: n=1531, n_treated=149, eff=0.226, p=0.289
Tamox ER+ postmeno: ER+ postmeno: n=21081, n_treated=6344, eff=0.010, p=0.807; ER+ premeno: ER+ premeno: n=13805, n_treated=4191, eff=0.044, p=0.377
Sacit HER2-low/ER-: HER2lo/ER-: n=5574, n_treated=574, eff=-0.035, p=0.716; HER2-low/ER+: HER2lo/ER+: n=12969, n_treated=1259, eff=-0.014, p=0.863
Pembro TNBC: TNBC: n=10496, n_treated=1578, eff=0.072, p=0.222; TNBC + high CRP: TNBC+hiCRP: n=5189, n_treated=819, eff=-0.008, p=0.918

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for prognostic confounders within ER+ patients, treatment_palbociclib still increases pfs_months by ~1.5 months on average.
After adjustment within ER+ patients, treatment_tamoxifen has an effect on pfs_months that is not distinguishable from zero.
After adjustment within HER2+ patients, treatment_trastuzumab has no detectable effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment within BRCA-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib has a small positive effect on pfs_months whose adjusted p>0.05.
After adjustment within HER2-low patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has no detectable effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment within TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab has no detectable beneficial effect on pfs_months.

Tamoxifen in ER+ adjusted: n=34886, n_treated=10535, adj_eff=0.003, adj_p=0.868; vs ER-: n=15114, n_treated=4500, adj_eff=0.001, adj_p=0.912
Trastuzumab in HER2+ adjusted: n=9060, n_treated=1890, adj_eff=0.012, adj_p=0.19; vs HER2-: n=40940, n_treated=8149, adj_eff=-0.016, adj_p=0.387
Olaparib in BRCA+ adjusted: n=2499, n_treated=239, adj_eff=0.061, adj_p=0.486; vs BRCA-: n=47501, n_treated=4707, adj_eff=-0.031, adj_p=0.133
Sacit in HER2-low adjusted: n=18543, n_treated=1833, adj_eff=0.047, adj_p=0.19; vs not HER2-low: n=31457, n_treated=3154, adj_eff=0.011, adj_p=0.643
Pembro in TNBC adjusted: n=10496, n_treated=1578, adj_eff=0.007, adj_p=0.46; vs not TNBC: n=39504, n_treated=6007, adj_eff=-0.052, adj_p=0.0132

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib benefit is similar in BRCA1+ and BRCA2+ patients (no statistically detectable difference between the two genes).
treatment_tamoxifen has no benefit in ER+/PR+ or ER+/PR- after adjustment.
treatment_palbociclib retains its large adjusted positive effect on pfs_months in the ER+/HER2- subgroup.
treatment_trastuzumab has no benefit in HER2+ regardless of ECOG.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has no benefit in HER2-low/ER- after adjustment.
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit (or harm) in TNBC does not differ by has_brain_mets.

analyses.result_summary: Olap BRCA1 only: n=1277, n_treated=120, adj_eff=0.182, adj_p=0.14; BRCA2 only: n=1222, n_treated=119, adj_eff=-0.043, adj_p=0.731
Tamox ER+/PR+ adjusted: n=29674, n_treated=8942, adj_eff=-0.005, adj_p=0.81; ER+/PR- adjusted: n=5212, n_treated=1593, adj_eff=0.044, adj_p=0.343
Palbo ER+/HER2- adjusted: n=28589, n_treated=9971, adj_eff=1.923, adj_p=0
Trast HER2+/ECOG<=1 adjusted: n=7748, n_treated=1620, adj_eff=0.006, adj_p=0.511; HER2+/ECOG=2 adjusted: n=1312, n_treated=270, adj_eff=0.058, adj_p=0.0496
Sacit HER2-low & ER- adjusted: n=5574, n_treated=574, adj_eff=0.012, adj_p=0.46
Pembro TNBC + brain mets: n=1056, n_treated=149, adj_eff=0.041, adj_p=0.267; TNBC no brain mets: n=9440, n_treated=1429, adj_eff=0.002, adj_p=0.875

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib has a three-way interaction with er_positive and her2_positive: benefit is largest in ER+/HER2- and smaller (or absent) when HER2+.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has a three-way interaction with her2_low and er_positive (largest benefit in HER2-low/ER-).
treatment_pembrolizumab has a three-way interaction with er_positive and her2_positive consistent with TNBC-restricted benefit.
treatment_olaparib has no three-way BRCA1 × BRCA2 interaction (effect is through either gene independently).
treatment_trastuzumab has no three-way interaction with her2_positive and node_positive.

analyses.result_summary: palbo × er_positive × her2_positive interaction: 3-way coef=-1.883, p=3.15e-223; tx×m1=1.925 (p=0); tx×m2=-0.026 (p=0.592)
sacit × her2_low × er_positive interaction: 3-way coef=0.099, p=0.264; tx×m1=-0.025 (p=0.739); tx×m2=-0.035 (p=0.522)
pembro × er_positive × her2_positive interaction: 3-way coef=0.063, p=0.491; tx×m1=-0.080 (p=0.0434); tx×m2=-0.001 (p=0.991)
olap × brca1 × brca2 interaction: 3-way coef=0.000, p=nan; tx×m1=0.201 (p=0.125); tx×m2=-0.030 (p=0.82)
trast × her2_positive × node_positive interaction: 3-way coef=0.021, p=0.786; tx×m1=0.017 (p=0.747); tx×m2=-0.043 (p=0.2)

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive scan of two-binary-feature subgroups will confirm that treatment_palbociclib has its largest, most significant subgroup effects within er_positive=1 and pr_positive=1 (and within pik3ca_mutation=0).
For treatment_olaparib, the strongest two-feature subgroups by p-value involve brca1_mutation=1.

For treatments without true biology-driven targets in this dataset (tamoxifen, trastuzumab, sacituzumab_govitecan, pembrolizumab), the smallest-p subgroups will not be biologically aligned and likely reflect chance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+ patients, treatment_palbociclib benefit is approximately uniform across age tertiles, ECOG categories, albumin tertiles, and LDH tertiles (i.e., effect is driven by ER status, not these continuous prognostics).
Within HER2+ patients, treatment_trastuzumab effect remains ~zero across all clinical strata.
Within BRCA-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib effect remains modest (<1 month) across age, ECOG, albumin, and LDH strata.
Within HER2-low patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan does not show benefit in any age/ECOG/albumin/LDH stratum.
Within TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab benefit does not vary materially by age/ECOG/albumin/LDH strata (i.e., no further enriched subgroup is evident).

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive search across all ordered pairs of binary features for treatment_palbociclib will identify er_positive=1 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 (or pr_positive=1 AND pik3ca_mutation=0) as the single largest-effect subgroup.
An exhaustive search across all binary feature pairs will not identify any subgroup with a >0.5-month positive treatment_trastuzumab effect at p<0.05.
An exhaustive search across all binary feature pairs will identify brca1_mutation=1 AND node_positive=1 as a positive-effect olaparib subgroup with effect >0.5 months.
For treatment_pembrolizumab, exhaustive subgroup search yields predominantly negative-effect (harm) subgroups in non-TNBC strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final candidate: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months (positive effect) in patients with er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0; effect is essentially zero outside this subgroup; treatment-by-subgroup interaction is highly significant.

Final candidate: treatment_olaparib has a small positive PFS effect in patients with brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1; effect is much weaker (and not significant after adjustment) outside the BRCA+ subgroup.

Final candidate: treatment_tamoxifen has no detectable PFS benefit in er_positive=1 patients in this cohort; the unadjusted within-ER+ effect is ~0 (p>0.4) and the interaction with ER status is not significant.
Final candidate: treatment_trastuzumab has no detectable PFS benefit in her2_positive=1 patients in this cohort.
Final candidate: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has no detectable PFS benefit in her2_low=1 patients (or in HER2-low/ER- subgroup) in this cohort.

Final candidate: treatment_pembrolizumab has no detectable PFS benefit in TNBC patients (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0); the overall pembrolizumab main effect is slightly negative and concentrated in non-TNBC, ECOG>=1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a joint model that includes all main effects, all six treatments, and all six biology-driven treatment × biomarker interaction terms (palbociclib × er_positive; tamoxifen × er_positive; trastuzumab × her2_positive; olaparib × BRCA1-or-BRCA2; sacituzumab × her2_low; pembrolizumab × TNBC), only the palbociclib × er_positive interaction will remain robustly significant (p<0.001) with a coefficient near +1.5 months.

In the same joint model, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets (negative), and albumin_g_dl (positive) will be the dominant prognostic main effects, each shifting expected pfs_months by >0.5 month per unit/level.
In the joint model, none of the other five treatments has a significant marginal main effect on pfs_months at p<0.05.

analyses.result_summary: Joint OLS R^2=0.812, n=50000. Treatment × biomarker interaction terms: treatment_palbociclib_x_er_positive: coef=1.561, p=0; treatment_tamoxifen_x_er_positive: coef=0.007, p=0.762; treatment_trastuzumab_x_her2_positive: coef=0.017, p=0.586; treatment_olaparib_x_brca_any: coef=0.021, p=0.78; treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan_x_her2_low: coef=0.052, p=0.119; treatment_pembrolizumab_x_tnbc: coef=0.052, p=0.118

Top prognostic main effects from joint model: ecog_ps: coef=-1.172, p=0; stage_iv: coef=-1.551, p=0; has_brain_mets: coef=-0.975, p=0; albumin_g_dl: coef=0.478, p=0; ldh_u_l: coef=-0.001, p=1.19e-18; her2_positive: coef=-0.570, p=2.82e-272; tnbc: coef=-0.379, p=1.76e-60; er_positive: coef=-0.204, p=4.03e-22; brca_any: coef=-0.049, p=0.0344

Treatment main effects from joint model (after their interaction terms are included): treatment_tamoxifen: coef=-0.006, p=0.742; treatment_palbociclib: coef=0.010, p=0.575; treatment_trastuzumab: coef=-0.017, p=0.215; treatment_olaparib: coef=-0.014, p=0.39; treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan: coef=-0.004, p=0.827; treatment_pembrolizumab: coef=-0.027, p=0.0768

proposed_hypotheses.text: Best-supported subgroup hypothesis for treatment_palbociclib: PFS benefit is concentrated in patients with er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0; in this triple-defined subgroup the unadjusted effect is approximately +2.9 months. The pik3ca_mutation=1 status appears to suppress the benefit even within ER+/HER2- patients.

Best-supported subgroup hypothesis for treatment_olaparib: small positive PFS effect (~+0.35 months) in patients with brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1; effect is not robust to adjustment but is the only directionally biologically plausible subgroup signal in the cohort.

Best-supported subgroup hypothesis for treatment_tamoxifen, treatment_trastuzumab, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, and treatment_pembrolizumab: NO biology-aligned subgroup with statistically supported PFS benefit was found. For each, the within-target-biomarker effect is ≤|0.1| months and not significant after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Refined triple subgroup test for palbociclib in er_positive=1 & her2_positive=0 & pik3ca_mutation=0: n=18681, n_treated=6571, mean_treated=7.209, mean_untreated=4.300, eff=2.908, p=0. Three-way interaction term palbociclib × er_positive × (1-pik3ca_mutation) coef=2.391, p=0 in adjusted model.

Within brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 (n=2499, n_treated=239), olaparib unadjusted eff=+0.346 (p=0.041); adjusted within-subgroup eff=+0.061 (p=0.49). Outside this subgroup eff=-0.056 (p=0.14). Interaction (in_subgroup × olaparib) coef=+0.086, p=0.36.

Tamoxifen ER+ adj eff=+0.003, p=0.87. Trastuzumab HER2+ adj eff=+0.012, p=0.19. Sacituzumab HER2-low adj eff=+0.047, p=0.19; HER2-low & ER- adj eff=+0.012, p=0.46. Pembrolizumab TNBC adj eff=+0.007, p=0.46. None reach a meaningful effect or significance threshold.
